text: Best Japanese food in Singapore? I’m open to anything — sushi, ramen, izakaya.
Any good ramen spots that aren’t a 2-hour queue? Prefer somewhere central (Orchard/CBD).

Where should I go for classic Cantonese in Singapore?

Where’s a solid Teochew restaurant for orh nee, braised duck, and cold crab?
Best dim sum in Singapore for a weekend lunch? Not too touristy.
Best North Indian (butter chicken / dal makhani) that’s consistent and not too oily?
Where can I get a proper South Indian thali (veg) with good sambar + dosa near Little India?
Any shiok Malay food places for ayam penyet / nasi padang that locals actually go to?

Best Italian restaurants in Singapore right now?
Where’s the best Korean BBQ in Singapore that doesn’t smell like smoke for 2 days after?

Best Vietnamese in Singapore for pho + banh mi — casual, quick, and actually good.

Any good date night restaurants in Singapore? Something with nice ambience.
Date night this Friday — romantic but not too atas, near Duxton Hill. Where should we go?
Anniversary dinner ideas: great food, good service, and feels special. Budget flexible.
Need a business lunch spot in the CBD that’s quiet enough to talk and not too slow.
Business dinner with a senior client — upscale, private-ish, and easy to get to from Raffles Place.
Family celebration restaurant where kids + grandparents will all be happy?
Where should we go for a long catch-up with old friends — good food, can talk, not rushed?
Any places that are great for solo dining (no awkward vibes), preferably at a counter?
First date, casual — somewhere in town where we can walk after. Not too loud. Suggestions?
Birthday dinner for my partner — a bit fancy, but not ‘stuffy fine dining’.
Team dinner for 12 people — somewhere lively with shareable dishes and easy reservations.
Friends visiting from overseas — where should I take them for a very ‘Singapore’ food experience?
Okay don’t judge — I need a breakup dinner spot. Comforting food, not too crowded, low-key.
I’m planning to propose. Need a restaurant with a romantic setting and staff who can help a bit discreetly.

After-gym dinner near Orchard: high-protein options, not greasy, and open late.
Taking my parents out — they like ‘proper’ restaurants with good service and not too spicy.
Small celebration (4 pax) — we want something special, good wine list, and can actually hear each other.
What are the best places to eat around Raffles Place/CBD on a weekday night?
I’m in Tiong Bahru this weekend — where should I eat that feels local but not just cafes?
Dempsey dinner: looking for a lush, chill spot for a long meal. What’s worth it there?
Where do people actually go for dinner in Holland V these days (not just drinking)?
Katong/Joo Chiat: where can I get a proper dinner + dessert crawl? Give me 2–3 stops.
Best eats in Chinatown that aren’t the obvious tourist picks?
Little India dinner: I want something spicy and legit, but still comfortable for bringing someone new to Indian food.
Any good food around Kampong Glam/Arab Street for an evening stroll?
Where should I eat around Orchard that isn’t a mall chain?
Sentosa lunch: somewhere with a view, decent food, and not a total tourist trap.
East Coast Park area — what’s good for a casual dinner after a walk?
Tanjong Pagar dinner ideas: mix of casual and slightly nicer, open past 9pm.
Keong Saik: suggest a vibey dinner spot for 2, plus a nearby place for dessert/drinks after.
What are your go-to restaurants around Duxton Hill for a chill dinner?
Robertson Quay: where’s a good spot for dinner by the river that isn’t overpriced?
Clarke Quay area: need a late-ish dinner that’s not just pub food.
Bukit Timah: where can I bring family for a nice weekend lunch?
Novena/Thomson: any hidden gems for dinner that are easy with MRT and not too crowded?
Looking for a romantic restaurant in Singapore — what would you recommend?
Romantic + low lighting + not too loud, and dessert must be good. Any ideas near town?
Any cozy, comforting places that feel like a hug? Good for rainy evenings.
Where’s a lively, buzzy place with great energy for a Friday night dinner?
I need somewhere quiet enough for a proper conversation — no blasting music. Any good picks?
Where can I eat somewhere I can people-watch (nice crowd, fun atmosphere)?
Any restaurants with outdoor seating that’s actually pleasant (not just sweating)?
Rooftop dinner with a view — but food must be legit, not just vibes. Suggestions?
Any hidden / speakeasy-ish spots where the entrance is discreet and the vibe is cool?
Need an Instagram-worthy place for a friend visiting — but please, food still needs to be good.
Old-school charm: think classic Singapore restaurant vibes. Where should I go?
Modern minimalist restaurant — clean design, calm, good plating. Where’s the vibe?
I want hawker-style food but air-conditioned. Any ‘kopitiam but comfy’ recs?
Late-night supper vibes after 11pm — not fast food. Where can we go?
Sunday brunch energy: bright, relaxed, good coffee/cocktails, not a sad buffet. Ideas?
Dinner with a bit of lounge music (not club loud). Somewhere you can still talk.
Any restaurants with lots of greenery / garden feel that’s not too atas?
Casual but hip — like you’d take a friend who’s picky and still impress them. Where’s good?
Where can I eat well in Singapore for under $20 per person?
Under $20pp near Raffles Place — sit-down lunch, fast, and actually tasty. Any recs?
Mid-range dinner ($30–60pp): what’s your best value pick right now?
I’ve got $50pp for dinner around Tiong Bahru — want something not boring. Suggestions?
If I’m splurging $100+ per person, what restaurants are truly worth it in Singapore?
I want a splurge dinner but not white-tablecloth stuffy. Great food, fun vibe. Where?
What’s the best value-for-money ‘atas’ meal in Singapore? Like feels fancy but wallet doesn’t cry.
Which popular restaurants are actually worth the price (and which aren’t)?
Cheap but actually good — not ‘cheap and sad’. Where’s reliably shiok?
Any omakase that’s good but not overpriced? I don’t need gold flakes, just quality.
Best set lunch deals in town on weekdays? Think $25–$40 and solid portions.
Recommend places with transparent pricing (no surprise service charge + tiny portions).
I’m vegetarian — what are the best restaurants in Singapore that won’t treat veg like an afterthought?
Any legit vegan spots that non-veg friends will still enjoy?
Halal restaurant recommendations in Singapore for a nice dinner (not just fast casual)?
Need gluten-free options for dinner — can you suggest places that take it seriously?
Kid-friendly restaurants with decent food for adults too. Any ideas?
Looking for a wheelchair-accessible restaurant with step-free entry and roomy seating.
I need a place that can take a group of 10–14 and won’t be a logistical nightmare.
Any restaurants with a private dining room for a business dinner (8–12 pax)?
Where can I eat after 10pm in Singapore — proper food, not just bar bites?
What good restaurants are open on Mondays? I keep finding places that are closed.
I hate planning — where can I walk in without a reservation and still get a great meal?
Any places that can do ‘no pork, no lard’ (for family) and still have lots of options?

Top 5 hawker or kopitiam spots that you’d recommend to someone who thinks SG food is only ‘chicken rice’.
Which is better for a special occasion: Odette or Les Amis? Why?
For BBQ: 2D1N or Super Star K? Which one is more worth it these days?
Seafood: Jumbo Seafood vs No Signboard — where should I bring visitors?
Is Burnt Ends still worth it, or is it mostly hype now?
Is Nouri worth the price if I liked the ‘storytelling’ kind of meal?
What restaurants in Singapore are overrated (and why)?
What are some underrated restaurants in Singapore that deserve more love?
Which Michelin-star restaurants in Singapore are actually worth going to?
Best roast meats (char siew / sio bak) in Singapore — give me your top 3.
Top 5 dinner spots around Duxton/Keong Saik, ranked from must-go to nice-to-have.
I’m visiting Singapore for 3 days. I want the full range — hawker, cafes, and 1 fine dining. Plan my meals.
I’ve got 2 nights in SG: one budget hawker night and one ‘treat yourself’ night. Recommend where to go and what to order.
My partner wants Thai, I want Italian. Where can we compromise without either of us feeling sad?
I like spicy, sour flavours and I’m not into sweet sauces. Where should I eat in Singapore?
I had an amazing meal at Nouri last year — what else would I like in Singapore with a similar ‘thoughtful’ vibe?
Where do Singapore chefs actually eat on their day off? Not the usual lists.
I’m new to SG but I don’t want tourist traps. Where do locals go for everyday ‘shiok’ meals?
Need a place that’s vegetarian-friendly, good for a date, and near an MRT. Any suggestions?
Family dinner with kids + grandparents, plus we want a nice view (rooftop/river). Where works best?
Plan a walkable food crawl: start at Chinatown, end near Clarke Quay. 3 stops, mix of sweet + savoury.
It’s 11pm, I’m in town, and I want supper that’s not greasy. Give me options that are open late.
I’m overwhelmed by options. Ask me 5 quick questions then recommend 3 restaurants based on my answers.
